objective.subject: Mathematics
objective.statement: Find and explain the area of rectangles and simple rectilinear shapes using square units and array structure.
misconceptions: Confuses area with perimeter by counting around the edge.
Counts grid lines instead of square units.
Uses length plus width instead of rows times columns for rectangles.
objective.parent_explanation: Can find the area of rectangles using square units and explain that area is inside space.
objective.teacher_evidence: Counts square units, uses rows and columns, and avoids perimeter confusion.

activities.title: I can find the area of a rectangle using square units.
activities.prompt: Cover the floor with equal square tiles.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Tap the inside space of the shape.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Separate area from perimeter before measuring.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Area is the inside space. Perimeter is around the edge.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Inside fill and outside edge toggle on a rectangle.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Cover the floor with equal square tiles.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Introduce area as counting square units.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: To measure area, cover the space with equal squares.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A workshop floor fills with square tiles.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: A 4 by 5 rectangle has 4 rows of 5 squares.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Connect counting squares to array multiplication.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Four rows of five square units makes twenty square units.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Rows and columns highlight on a 4 by 5 grid.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Fill the rectangle and count the square units.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Practise complete tiling without gaps or overlaps.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Learner drags square tiles into a rectangular grid.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Which answer is area, not perimeter?
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Area counts inside squares, not the outside edge.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Inside tiles and outside border compete as two answer paths.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Teach Rex why length plus width is not the area.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: I added the length and width. Why did I miss some squares?
teaching_sequence.visual_model: The companion counts only one row and one column before repair.

vocabulary.child_explanation: The amount of flat space a shape covers.
vocabulary.audio_script: Area is the space covered inside the shape.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A rectangle filled with glowing squares.

vocabulary.child_explanation: One equal square used to measure area.
vocabulary.audio_script: Each little square is one square unit.

vocabulary.child_explanation: The distance around the edge of a shape.
vocabulary.audio_script: Perimeter is around the outside, not the inside.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A line tracing the outside edge.

manipulative.purpose: Represent area as covered square units and connect rectangular grids to multiplication.
manipulative.interaction_notes: Learners fill grids, highlight rows/columns, compare area and perimeter, and explain square-unit reasoning.
manipulative.accessibility_notes: Keyboard fill controls, high-contrast grid lines and reduced-motion static fills.

adaptive_support.low_sensory: Use calm grid fills and no flashing tiles.
adaptive_support.reduced_motion: Use instant tile fill and static row highlights.
adaptive_support.audio_first: Read dimensions and square-unit prompts aloud.
adaptive_support.reading_support: Use labelled rows, columns and square units.
adaptive_support.attention_support: Separate fill, count and multiply steps.
adaptive_support.processing_support: Allow tile recount and row/column toggles.
adaptive_support.confidence_support: Treat perimeter answers as a useful comparison to repair.
adaptive_support.eal_support: Preteach area, perimeter, inside, edge, row, column and square unit.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Distinguish inside area from outside perimeter.
Count complete square units in rectangles.
Use rows times columns to find rectangle area.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: length x width grid + multiplication link

variant_blueprints.purpose: Find area of simple rectilinear shapes by decomposing into rectangles.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: L-shape grid + split line + count regions

variant_blueprints.purpose: Maintain area/perimeter distinction and rectangle area facts.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Confuses area with perimeter by counting around the edge.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: Learner chooses edge count when asked for area.
repair_sequence: Ask learner to choose inside or edge for a new shape.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner selects and measures inside square units.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Uses length plus width instead of rows times columns for rectangles.
repair_sequence: Show each row has the same number of squares.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner uses rows times columns on a new rectangle.

feedback.companion_prompt: Teach Rex why length plus width is not the area.
feedback.parent_summary: Can find the area of rectangles using square units and explain that area is inside space.
feedback.teacher_evidence: Counts square units, uses rows and columns, and avoids perimeter confusion.
next_actions: Interleave with perimeter after area is secure.
Use area models to support multiplication.

body.prompt: Find the area of a rectangle with 4 rows of 5 square units.

questions.explanation: The area is 20 square units because 4 x 5 = 20.

body.prompt: Which measurement tells the inside space covered by a shape?

questions.explanation: Area measures the inside space a shape covers.

body.prompt: A rectangle is 3 squares by 6 squares. What is its area?

hints: Count square spaces, not grid lines.

reward_payload.explanation: Can find the area of rectangles using square units and explain that area is inside space.
reward_payload.companion_prompt: Teach Rex why length plus width is not the area.
